headline: Green Building Standard Covers Only 4% of New Developments
context: CIDB data shows only 4% of new commercial and residential projects in 2024 achieved Green Building Index certification despite government incentives launched in 2009.

cards.big: Malaysia introduced green building incentives 15 years ago.
cards.sub: After 15 years, 96% of new buildings still do not meet the standard. The incentive has failed.

cards.big: GBI-certified buildings use 35% less energy on average.
cards.sub: Over a 30-year lifecycle, savings exceed certification costs by a factor of 4.2.

cards.big: Singapore mandates green standards for all new buildings since 2008.
cards.sub: Malaysia's voluntary approach has produced 4% adoption versus Singapore's 95%.

cards.big: Tax incentives cover only 3% of additional green construction costs.
cards.sub: Developers cite the remaining 97% gap as the reason for non-adoption.

cards.big: A voluntary standard adopted by 4% after 15 years is a voluntary failure.
The evidence from Singapore, the EU, and Japan is clear: green building works when mandatory. Malaysia's voluntary approach has run its 15-year experiment. The results are in.
